title: 5 Logging in to Enterprise Fraud Management
body_text:
5	Logging in to Enterprise Fraud Management
5.1	Overview of Logging in to Enterprise Fraud Management
Access to Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) is granted through password login on Central 1âs Client Centre. You must be set up as a CPS Admin Security Officer or as a Client Centre User with access to EFM. See the User Management Part or the CPS Admin Application Part for procedures on creating Security Officers, Client Centre Users, and assigning access to EFM.
Note:	EFM users require a 2-step security token (hard token or soft token) to access the Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) solution. See the User Management Part for procedures on assigning a new token or updating an existing token.
Related Information
Training Videos:
Fraud Alert Services in EFM
Training Videos:
User Management
and
2-Step Security
5.2	Logging in to Enterprise Fraud Management
To log in to Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM):
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
.
Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
EFM (Enterprise Fraud Management)
. See Figure 3.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youâve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under âMy Favouritesâ.
Figure 3: Accessing EFM
Enter your email address and select
Sign In
.
See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Signing in to EFM
Enter the code that is generated on your registered 2-step security token and select
Done
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Entering Your 2-Step Security Code
Note:	See the 2-Step Security Part for information on installing and registering your token or view the 2-Step Security training videos.
After successful validation, youâll land on the Dashboard for Fraud Alert Services in EFM. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Dashboard / Landing Page for Fraud Alert Services in EFM
Related Information
Training Videos:

and
